body.title: UC session_id canonicalization — strip bare/URI mismatch dropping all Igor replies
body.result: UC canonicalizes session_id to comms:// URI at transport boundary (agent_send entry, _ws_endpoint init, join_session ingress). 60/60 UC-family tests pass. Commit ccb007ad.
body.description:
Observed live: Igor generates replies but the web UI never receives them. Root cause: browser joins session with comms://shared URI form (currentChannel in the tab UI), Igor/agent_send default session_id='shared' (bare form). Server keys _session_clients/_session_history on the literal string, so agent_send writes to 'shared' while browser subscribes 'comms://shared' → fanout=0 DROP every reply.

**Fix:** UC transport boundary canonicalizes to comms:// URI form (matches browser + lab/utility_closet/comms.py channel registry). _canonical_session_id() helper applied at agent_send entry, _ws_endpoint initial current_session, and join_session new_sid ingress. HTTP POST flows through agent_send so no extra change needed there.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/utility_closet_server.py (helper + 3 call sites), tests/test_uc_delivery_telemetry.py + tests/test_utility_closet_server.py (update assertions that keyed on bare 'shared').

**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not. No new DB schemas. HIGH-inertia file NOT touched (this is UC transport, not brainstem).

**Scope boundary:** IN — session_id canonicalization at UC boundary + test updates to match new key form. OUT — changing comms.py's URI scheme, changing the browser's channel-tab UI, modifying agent_send callers in Igor (they keep using bare names; UC coerces).

**Test plan:** Updated 2 existing unit tests (test_agent_send_logs_entry, test_agent_send_to_unknown_session_produces_drop, test_agent_send_writes_to_history). All UC-family suites pass (test_uc_delivery_telemetry, test_utility_closet_server, test_utility_closet_client, test_comms, test_comms_default_channels = 60 tests). Live verification: restart UC, check that Igor's next reply fanout>0 in uc_deliver logs.
